title: trifolium vesiculosum subsp. mutabile
intro: Trifolium vesiculosum subsp. mutabile, also known as the variable clover, is a subspecies of the vesicular clover plant, belonging to the Fabaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds in its leaves and flowers. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help in alleviating conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. Traditionally, variable clover is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, although further research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety.
